final_response:
I apologize, but I don't have permission to run systemd commands in this environment as there's no interactive user available. This is a limitation of the current sandbox environment.

However, I can show you the common ways to view systemd services and their status on a Linux system:

**To list all services and their status, you would normally use:**

```bash
# List all unit files and their status
systemctl list-unit-files --type=service

# List all running services only
systemctl list-units --
